name: Chunk 2/6
text:
# Large British  Building Society  
Migrating customer data and FC/RC feeds to SAP, ending Unisys reliance  
The building society had embarked on a strategic digital transformation initiative focused on modernising legacy Customer Information Systems (CIS), originally hosted on Mainframe platforms, by transitioning to a scalable and efficient modern data lakehouse architecture. This migration is part of a broader effort to enhance data accessibility, improve operational efficiency, and support for various business units and data consumers. IBM Consulting was engaged to support the programme for:  
1. Data Modeling and Transformation using DBT and SQL, Designing and developing modular, reusable, and version-controlled DBT (Data Build Tool) models.Ensuring data quality and consistency through rigorous testing, documentation, and data validation frameworks within DBT.
2. Data Processing and Workflow Automation using DBT and Databricks, Implementing performance-optimized ETL jobs to process raw data into curated layers (bronze/L0, gold/L2) aligned with lakehouse architecture principles.
3. Orchestration using Azure Data Factory (ADF) and Control-M
4. Data Delivery and Consumer Integration - Generating standardized data extract files (CSV, TXT, etc.) tailored for different business consumers and external systems.  
Outcome and Business Impact  
This project enables the decommissioning of legacy CIS systems, reducing infrastructure costs and technical debt. It provides a modern, cloud-native data lake house architecture that improves data accessibility, reliability, and capabilities across the organization. The migration also lays a strong foundation for advanced analytics, machine learning, and self-service BI initiatives in future.  
© 2023 IBM Corporation - Confidential  
IBM Consulting  
Key Success Factors:  
- Reduced Operational Costs: Decommissioning legacy mainframe systems significantly lowers infrastructure and maintenance costs, freeing up budget for innovation and modernization Embed the right capacity & capability
- Enhanced Data Accessibility & Agility: Migrating to a modern lakehouse architecture enables faster, more flexible access to customer data across departments, improving decision-making and responsiveness.
- Improved Data Quality & Compliance: Centralized, well-governed data pipelines with built-in validation and auditing ensure higher data accuracy, consistency, and regulatory compliance.
- Scalable Foundation for Advanced Analytics: The new architecture supports real-time processing and machine learning workloads, accelerating digital transformation and enabling future AI-driven initiatives.  
The picture is a technical diagram illustrating the "RC/Non-RC Architecture."  
The diagram is segmented into five major areas: **Source**, **ETL**, **CIS - Lakehouse**, **Consumer**, and **Code**.  
**Source** depicts various "Inbound Files," including examples like "File," "Personal," and "Mortgage," which feed into "Azure ADLS / Blob."  
The **ETL** phase utilizes "Azure Data Factory."  
The **CIS - Lakehouse** is the central processing area. Data flows through this system, utilizing "Databricks Workflows / Jobs." The architecture layers include the **L0 Layer** and the **L2 Layer**, with a note stating "Intermediate Tables are not Permanent Tables." The data eventually reaches the "Final Extract Store Azure ADLS / Blob." A key text box specifies the "Delta Lake Mediation Architecture with only L0, L2 Layers," and notes that "Azure Datakale Storage is underlying storage system for entire lakehouse."  
The **Consumer** section shows data accessed via an "API" that connects to a "Target System."  
The **Code** section shows the workflow starting with a "Developer" and using `dbt` (data build tool) to interact with a "git Code Repository."  
A dedicated **Platform** section lists several underlying Azure services: "Azure Key Vault," "Azure Monitor," "Azure DevOps," and "Databricks Unity Catalog."  
The picture is a photograph of a busy, modern office or call center environment. The main focus is a blonde woman who is seated at a desk, wearing a headset, and looking down as if focused on a computer screen. She is surrounded by the working environment, with multiple monitors displaying data and graphs in the background. The background shows several other colleagues at their stations, creating a sense of corporate activity. No clearly visible text or words are detectable in the picture.  
# Large British  Building Society  
This organisation needed to migrate data assets from Teradata vantage to Lakehouse (Databricks) by first quarter of FY 2026-27 (i.e. Jun-2026).  
IBM Consulting was engaged to optimise and unify the data landscape across the organization by consolidating all data assets onto a strategic technology platform—Microsoft Azure. Subject Matter Experts from IBM were brought in to evaluate the data platform migration strategy and architecture, ensuring alignment with aggressive project timelines. A collaborative workshop was conducted in Pune, bringing together client stakeholders and IBM SMEs for hands-on sessions and direct access to the Acquire and Publish framework. IBM team was recognized for their proactive approach in delivering the Workstream Medallion Architecture Bronze milestone and L1-L2 deliverables ahead of schedule.  
A key advantage was the development of GenAI assets during the conversion of Teradata procedures to DBT. IBM’s value proposition was focused on ICA-DMA assets, which is currently being deployed in the client’s environment. This significantly accelerated code development and testing, enabling faster project delivery and improved time-to-value.  
The data platform migration strategy necessitated the deployment of IBM assets within the client’s network, a process currently underway. POC was executed using a complex data mart codebase, demonstrating a significant acceleration in code development and testing. This validated the asset-driven approach as a key enabler for faster project delivery and improved time-to-value.  
© 2023 IBM Corporation - Confidential  
>30% reduction in code build and test activities.  
Increased efficiency & productivity which helped to fast track the project delivery and meet the stringent timelines.  
IBM Consulting  
Key Success Factors:  
- Use of IBM GenAI assets DMA(anticipated post deployment of assets in client environment)
- Engage business users early and often
- Embed the right capacity & capability into the data access, extraction and preparation process
- Priorities upfront architectural design with a view to the future

name: Chunk 3/6
text:
# Case Study for a UK based Bank  
1. Compute data lineage for CDEs across in scope functional areas for Eco Crime—establishing baseline maturity
2. Measure data quality for CDEs across in scope functional areas for Eco Crime—establishing baseline maturity
3. Review technical data reconciliation processes for Payments Screening—identifying gaps & immediate steps  
Compute data lineage  
Measure data quality  
Review data reconciliation  
Promote capability build  
- Consulted c. 150+ SMEs across business and technology and reviewed documentation for 78 systems across 6 “in scope” areas
- Created 6 dataflow diagrams and 108 interface contracts to visualise flow of 205 Critical Data Elements  
- Derived technical rules from data lineage metadata and worked with 7 Business SMEs to create 400+ DQ rules and SSDs for 205 CDEs
- Configuring SAS Tool to run quality checks against 2 DQ dimensions (Conformity & Completeness)  
- Interviewed c. 20 SMEs for Payments Screening to produce 1 maturity assessment report on as-is technical data reconciliation
- Working with NW to identify additional review area and next steps for P.S. based on findings  
- Produced 1 comprehensive XLS mapping file of functional areas, CDEs, interfaces, and SMEs across business and technology to augment information in Planview
- Actively coaching BAU colleagues to ensure successful handover  
The picture is a detailed data flow or process mapping diagram titled "Analysis And MI Reporting."  
The diagram illustrates how data is sourced, processed, and aggregated from multiple core systems into a final reporting stage.  
**Input Data Sources (Left Side):**
Data originates from several initial systems: Payments, Unumis, SAP, Sirs, Chaps, and CIS. These streams flow into a central component labeled FCD.  
**Processing and Core Systems (Center):**
The FCD then processes data through various intermediary systems, including BASE24, IFM, Portman, TSYS, and FCRM. These systems interact with specialized modules such as RFS Personal Loans, RFS Personal Loans MIS, and Chaps.  
**Analytical and Specialized Feeds (Right Side):**
Data proceeds into four major analytical blocks:
1.  **RFS Personal Loans:** Processes data leading to the RFS Data Set.
2.  **SAS Analytics (AML TXM):** Handles Anti-Money Laundering (AML) transactions, generating the AML Data Set.
3.  **CAW HDS:** Processes data streams to produce the HDS Data Set.
4.  **NetReveal:** Generates the NetReveal Data Set.  
**Final Reporting:**
The various data sets (RFS Data Set, RCD Data Set, CHAPS Data Set, HDS Data Set, AML Data Set, and NetReveal Data Set) converge at the final reporting step, shown under the title "Analysis And MI Reporting."

This image is a text-based corporate report or presentation slide titled "FCTP Data Reconciliation Initiative - Current State and Key Findings."  
Under the main title, the document also includes the section headers "Current State" and "Key Facts."  
**Current State:**
The bullet points under "Current State" detail the following points:
*   IBM has been engaged on a Time and Materials contract to assess the existing technical data reconciliation process(es) for Payment Screening.
*   We have conducted 30+ meetings with 19 SMEs over multiple weeks as well as have studied client-provided documentation, including "Payment Screening Data Reconciliation BRD" (July 12, 2023).
*   The following points summarize our specific observations from discussions with SMEs regarding Data Reconciliation along with the documents we reviewed: Lack of evidence to suggest transaction movements and data screening across various systems have been monitored or reconciled as regards the CDES within Payment Screening.
*   Data Reconciliation for Payment Screening does not appear to have been implemented yet although multiple SMEs confirmed this is a relevant and active pain point for the organisation.

name: Chunk 5/6
text:
*   None of the 19 SMEs consulted could identify the overarching Data Reconciliation process employed by Nationwide from a Data Governance perspective, specifically for Regulatory Compliance. This includes specific frameworks, guiding principles, or even an example of a similar end-to-end data reconciliation process conducted for another transaction management process.
*   Further improvement needed across Nationwide on how best to define Data Reconciliation policies, processes and governance framework as multiple instances of disconnected observed.
*   Apparent lack of action plan and strategy to address 3rd Party Audit report findings about absence of data reconciliation process in Payment Screening area to confirm monitoring of all transactional activities across all customers, products, lines of business, and transaction types within Nationwide.  
**Key Facts:**
The bullet points under "Key Facts" include:
*   Information gathered from various meetings with FCTP screening Delivery leads.
*   Referred artifacts "FCTP - Screening - Decision" in Confluence, dated July 12, 2023. As "Payment Screening: No reconciliation / evidence exists to confirm that all of the payments that need to be screened by HLS are sent to the various payment systems across NOBS" (Ongoing Data Lineage exercise revealed connection between various payment services like Chips, Swift, SEPA, Fast Payment, Visa Fund Fast Funds and Filter Saver (HLS). However, it does not unveil the following:
*   If the products across channels are sending all the required transaction to HLS.
*   What are the checks and balances in place across different 'hops' all the way to HLS and back.
*   In the Screening functional area, titled "Payment Screening Data Reconciliation BRD", it is indicated that the scope is yet to be defined, specifically highlighting a "Value to Value" comparison from the Source System to the Target System.

Business Control  (Sample)  
The ‘Country of Origin’  data element must be maintained in its original state  from the point of entry  into the system  through to  its utilization in  the risk rating system  within the CDD system. Any modification, alteration , or update  to this data element is  strictly prohibited  across all intermediary  systems and stages  of data processing.  
FCTP CDE “Country Of Origin” as derived from CDD (Customer Due Diligence) output  
Data Reconciliation Controls and Checks  
CDE Level : Customer data transmission is deemed successfully completed only when  
1. The number of customer records with ‘Country of  Origin’ matches between source and target
2. For every customer record the ‘Country of Origin’ field matches between source and target (String / checksum matching )
3. For every customer record , the format and data type of  ‘Country of origin’ field has not undergone any changes (like ‘Germany’ to  ‘Deutschland’ OR Country name to ISO or vice versa)
4. Invalid  ‘Country of  Origin’  should be flagged and  target system should be informed about those  
Interface Level  Key Controls (applicable for all CDEs):  
1. Every interface need to have job control (system audit, error threshold , altering & restart process)  implemented (either as in-built construct or post job process)
2. In the event of  any data or count mismatch (exceeding the threshold), the interface  must halt data loading and trigger a alert.
3. A detailed log should be maintained for every loading  operation with issues separately highlighted for resolution.  
Legend  
Reconciliation Control Points
